text: Storm snapped a branch on the beefsteak tomato. Used painter's tape to splint the minor break and removed the branch that was hanging/unsalvageable. Holding off on fertilizing until it recovers from the stress.
Noticed fungus gnats around the tomato soil. Started treatment: letting soil dry out more between waterings, put out yellow sticky traps, did a hydrogen peroxide drench to kill larvae in the soil.
Oregano is confirmed ready to harvest. Smells strong and leaves are full-sized.
Cut lower leaf stems still yellowing post water-soluble and regular fertilizer. Help to prevent disease and reduce likelihood of soil fungus, common on seacoast and with mix of old/new soil.
